title: juncus heldreichianus subsp. heldreichianus
intro: Juncus heldreichianus subsp. heldreichianus, also known as Heldreich's rush or Greek rush, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Juncaceae and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In traditional herbal medicine, Juncus heldreichianus subsp. heldreichianus is used to treat various health conditions, such as urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and respiratory problems, and is also valued for its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and can also be used in tincture or salve form to support its therapeutic benefits.
